title: Top defense stocks fly bull flags with higher prices in sight

title: Israel–Hamas war boosts U.S. defense ETFs, but why it won't help stocks for long
text: Hello! This is MarketWatch reporter Isabel Wang bringing you this week's ETF Wrap. In this week's edition, we look at defense ETFs, which have rallied on the back of the Middle East conflict since the Palestinian militant group launched surprise cross-border raids from Gaza 13 days ago, pushing the region to the precipice of a dangerous abyss.

title: 3 Space Stocks That Are Shooting for the Stars
text: Investing in space stocks is no longer science fiction. In fact, Morgan Stanley is forecasting that the global space industry could be valued at over $1 trillion by 2040.

title: Visa, Fiserv and other payment stocks have analysts playing defense
text: Wedbush analysts continue to see overall volume growth rates for payment stocks in the third quarter moderating on a year-over-year basis, while being relatively "flattish" quarter over quarter.  In a note to clients, they highlighted a number of relevant trends including consistent growth moderation in credit purchases as well as credit transactions, a likely reduction in large ticket (discretionary) item sales, continued declines in average ticket prices (credit cards); as well as moderating quarter over quarter growth in travel volumes.

title: Will Higher Expenses at Collins Aerospace Hurt RTX Q3 Earnings?
text: RTX Q3 results are likely to reflect the benefits of growing commercial air traffic. However, higher expenses at Collins Aerospace as well as Pratt & Whitney might have hurt the company's overall earnings performance.

title: What the Israel-Hamas war means for defense stocks
text: When war breaks out, defense companies tend to make money. That means aerospace and defense stocks tend to rise during geopolitical unrest.
